input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML — use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension:**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#AAAAAA}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework reads the device's current theme and picks `Light` or `Dark` automatically — no event wiring needed. Changes at the OS level are reflected immediately.

---

**C# equivalent — use `SetAppTheme` or `SetAppThemeColor`:**

```csharp
// For any BindableProperty:
var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Colors.White);

// For non-color properties use the generic version:
var page = new ContentPage();
page.SetAppTheme(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
                 Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),
                 Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));
```

`SetAppThemeColor(property, light, dark)` is the color shorthand; `SetAppTheme<T>(property, light, dark)` works for any type.

---

**Key points:**

| Aspect | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Follows `Application.RequestedTheme` (`Light` / `Dark` / `Unspecified`) |
| **Live updates** | Yes — switching the OS theme updates bound values instantly |
| **Scope** | Works on any `BindableProperty` (colors, images, strings, etc.) |
| **Resource alternative** | You can also define `AppThemeBinding` inside a `ResourceDictionary` style for reuse across pages |

**Reusable style example:**

```xml
<Application.Resources>
    <Style TargetType="Label" x:Key="ThemedLabel">
        <Setter Property="TextColor"
                Value="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />
    </Style>
</Application.Resources>
```

Then apply with `Style="{StaticResource ThemedLabel}"` on any label.
